address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

msContents.summary: A composite volume containing the Ziyárat-náma, the Lawḥ-i-Náṣir, the Dalá'il-i-Sab'a ("Seven Proofs"), and a miscellany of Bábí poems, written for Browne by a Bábí telegraph-clerk at Kirmán in July-August 1888. The poems are written at the end of the volume in double oblique lines, with occasional extra couplets inscribed between.

msItem.title: Ziyārahʹnāmah
زياره نامه
msItem.note: The "Book of Visitation" (cf. Gobineau's "Journal du Pèlerinage"), composed by the Báb at the beginning of his mission. Browne discussed this identification at pp. 896-902 of his second JRAS article on the Bábís (1889), there arguing it is identical with a work described by Mírzá Kazem-Beg in the Journal Asiatique, series 6, vol. 8, pp. 498-502. In a letter dated 1 October 1889, Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel confirmed the work is by the Báb and was written after the Manifestation.
msItem.quote: کتاب زيارت که أشاره فرموده اند از حضرت نقطه أست و بعد از ظهور است وعبارات أن شاهد است وزيارات بسيار نوشته اند

listBibl.bibl: See sub classmark BBP. 7 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Jul., 1892): 433-499.

msItem.title: Short epistle
msItem.note: Browne states the work "appears from internal evidence to have been written by one of those who claimed to manifest God," and that although he "cannot confidently decide" between the Báb and Bahá'u'lláh, he considers the latter more probable, in which case the "brother" addressed is probably Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel.
msItem.incipit: هو الباقی الفرد الرفیع... (f. 47a; Persian part begins f. 47b, l. 1)
msItem.explicit: ... اللهم فافرق بيننا و بينهم الى يوم بقائى

listBibl.bibl: See sub classmark BBP. 7 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Jul., 1892): 433-499.

msItem.title: Lawḥ-i naṣīr
لوح نصير
msItem.note: Per Browne, B. ii, pp. 949-953 and 1004. The Bábís of Kirmán told Browne this Tablet was composed by Bahá'u'lláh at Adrianople, and that in it he first formulated his claim. Browne notes he later learned from Baron Rosen of a second copy at St. Petersburg.

listBibl.bibl: See sub classmark BBP. 7 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Jul., 1892): 433-499.

msItem.title: Dalāʼil sabʻah
دلائل سبعه
The Seven Proofs
msItem.note: Per Browne, B. i, pp. 912-918 and 1001-2. In a letter of 1 October 1889, Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel affirmed the work as the Báb's "if it has not suffered interpolation," composed in the "Mountain of Mím" (Máh-Kú) - most of the Báb's books being revealed during his imprisonment at Máh-Kú and Chihríq, the rest at Shíráz and Iṣfahán. Browne states he took a copy to Cyprus and had it checked and endorsed as genuine by Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel personally, who added that it was written by the Báb for his amanuensis, Áqá Sayyid Ḥusayn of Yazd.
msItem.quote: دلائل سبعه از حضرت نقطه است هرگاه مداخلة در آن نباشد و در اپاجا نسخة آن نيست

listBibl.bibl: See sub classmark BBP. 7 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Jul., 1892): 433-499.

msItem.title: Several Bābī poems
msItem.note: A masnaví poem attributed to Qurratu'l-'Ayn (briefly described by Browne in B. ii, p. 1002); a ghazal also attributed to Qurratu'l-'Ayn, published with Browne's translation at pp. 314-316 of vol. ii of his Traveller's Narrative; a short poem of which Baron Rosen quotes the first bayt in Collections Scientifiques, vi, p. 251 (11 bayts given by Browne in full, with an English translation in the Appendix to his B. ii, pp. 823-5); another poem of 18 couplets in the same rhyme and metre; and a longer poem or group of poems combining tarkíb-band and tarjí'-band form, which Browne describes as having "very wild" language and "obscure" phraseology and allusions.

listBibl.bibl: See sub classmark BBP. 7 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts (Continuation)", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Jul., 1892): 433-499.

layoutDesc.layout: 10 lines to the page throughout the prose portions; the poems at the end are written in double oblique lines.

handDesc.handNote: Browne gives no description of the hand/script beyond layout for this item.

history.provenance: Written for E. G. Browne by a Bábí telegraph-clerk at Kirmán, Persia, in July-August 1888.
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: "Manuscript description based on. R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932. ", abbreviated by the OCIMCO project team; provenance expanded from E. G. Browne, "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bábī Manuscripts," Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, Jul. 1892, pp. 444-449.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions). Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Draft checked and updated with additional encoding and authority control by Yasmin Faghihi.

Claude (merge) Merged content from the corresponding Browne press-mark draft record (babi_ms/original_b_nos/BBP_7.xml) into this Fihrist record: summary, per-item notes and quotations, physical description, hand note, and provenance history rewritten from Browne's 1892 JRAS catalogue account (pp. 444-449). Item 2's authorship note preserves Browne's own uncertainty (the Báb or Bahá'u'lláh) while retaining this record's existing "anonymous" author assignment. Persian/Arabic quotations are unproofed OCR (Tesseract), flagged cert="low". Person/place authority keys reused from elsewhere in the collection where already established (E. G. Browne, Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel); left blank for Qurratu'l-'Ayn, who has no existing Fihrist/VIAF key in this collection. Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer.
Julian Cook Assigned role(s) to every person having an id (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Julian Cook Changed person and work ids (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Andrew Morrison Updated key attributes using update-keys.xsl
Andrew Morrison Adjusted summary using add-summaries2.xsl
Andrew Morrison Added summary using add-summaries.xsl
Andrew Morrison Removed origDate tags around something which is not a date using fix-dates.xsl
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/bodleian/fihrist-mss/tree/master/processing/convertFihrist2Bodley.xsl
